hero.title: The Mermaid Mask Wiki
hero.description: Solve Captain Mortuga's locked-room murder with Detective Grimoire and Sally. Explore the Mortuga submarine, inspect 3D clues, question suspects, and master every puzzle.

gameFeature.title: The Mermaid Mask Investigation
gameFeature.description: Solve a hand-painted locked-room murder aboard the Mortuga submarine. Inspect rotating 3D clues, question an eccentric crew, and rebuild the case through evidence-backed deductions.

theMermaidMaskWalkthrough.title: The Mermaid Mask Walkthrough
theMermaidMaskWalkthrough.subtitle: Follow the mandatory investigation route from Stonetop Inn to the final confrontation aboard the Mortuga submarine. Each section stops before revealing later story twists.
theMermaidMaskWalkthrough.intro: The Mermaid Mask is structured around an opening investigation and the main murder case, The Curse in the Cauldron. Use this route to collect required evidence, unlock every area, question the full crew, and recover when the next progression trigger is unclear.

steps.objective: Review the case and wake Sally.
actions: Inspect The Hex of the Helmsmen book lying near Sally.
Open the red Case File and read the locked-room murder summary.
Interact with Sally to begin the journey to the submarine.
steps.trigger: Leave the inn after Sally joins Grimoire.

steps.objective: Find the transport arranged by Captain Seafoam.
actions: Inspect the square, signs, fountain, and unusual green light.
Talk to Sally after examining the glowing area.
Wait for the Mortuga submarine to surface and board it.
steps.trigger: Enter the submarine from the Fore Deck.

steps.objective: Enter the ship and restore the first interior light.
actions: Inspect the glowing carved buoy on the Fore Deck.
Enter the Parlor and let Sally operate the lever that lights the pink flame.
Read the warning attached to the Control Room window.
Note that the Control Room and murder floor are initially inaccessible.
steps.trigger: Move through the available corridor toward the Mess Hall.

actions: Inspect the cards, cookware, lamps, candles, and cabinet.
Treat each shape as its number of sides.
Set the four displays to triangle for 3, square plus pentagon for 9, triangle plus square for 7, and square for 4.
steps.trigger: Finish inspecting the opened cabinet and continue to the Illusion Theatre.

actions: Inspect the ragged cloth connected to The Stargazing Girl.
Examine the upside-down illusion poster.
Inspect the chalk outline, the ancient stone cauldron, and the surrounding stage.
Review every highlighted point on the cauldron before opening the deduction.
steps.trigger: Complete the first case conclusion.

actions: Select Magnus Mortuga as the person who unlocked the cauldron.
Select the conclusion that he was killed by what was inside it.
Confirm the deduction to begin the main investigation.
steps.trigger: Chapter One, The Curse in the Cauldron, begins.

steps.objective: Solve the doll portrait and obtain the Glass Eyeball.
actions: Read the newspaper about the Lady of the Looking Glass and the sea-serpent report on its reverse.
Combine the six portrait cards into three complete girls.
Place Patricia in the center with the medium-sized doll.
Use the enlarging and shrinking lenses on the outer dolls.
Place the fish-eye lenses over the faces of the left and right girls.
steps.trigger: Collect the Glass Eyeball from the completed mechanism.

actions: Return to the Parlor and inspect the watching eyes near the Control Room door.
Go back to the Child's Room.
Interact with the blinking painting to reveal Symmetry Silkmoth.
Ask Symmetry about herself, the submarine, the crew, and every available clue.
steps.trigger: Return to the Control Room after completing Symmetry's initial interview.

steps.objective: Meet Sinthia Seafoam and recover the Captain's Log.
actions: Question Sinthia about Mortuga, her new command, Symmetry, and the other crew members.
Inspect the Submarine Blueprint, wheel, captain's jacket, and control equipment.
Connect the red cable from socket 1 to 9, blue from 3 to 11, and yellow from 6 to 12.
Take the Captain's Log from the opened compartment.
Present the new evidence to Sinthia before leaving.
steps.trigger: The bunk rooms and additional crew interviews become part of the active route.

steps.objective: Open the pig safe and search Sinthia's bunk.
actions: Read the Movie Magazine.
Set the pig-safe number network so the top branches read 3, 4, 1 and total 8, while the second branches read 2, 3, 1 and total 6.
Align the lower totals as 5, 9, and 2.
Take the gloomy underwater photograph from the safe.
Show the photograph to Sinthia and Symmetry.
Return to Sinthia's mattress after Symmetry points out the concealed material.
steps.trigger: Collect the Inn Deeds hidden beneath the mattress.

steps.objective: Interview Dirk Dansom and J.D. Wirman.
actions: Ask both men about themselves, Mortuga, the submarine, and the entire suspect list.
Solve Dirk's phone lock by making every connected channel begin and end with matching symbols.
Read the unlocked calls, messages, and emails.
Rotate the typewriter's inner and outer rings so the five displayed patterns follow the required 45-degree transformations.
steps.trigger: Recover the handwritten letter addressed to Wirman from Ibis.

actions: Ask Zephyr about Mortuga, his dream work, and every crew member.
Inspect the Hex Bottle, Dream Helmet, Fog Ball, and other interactive equipment.
Rotate and open each 3D clue until every inspection point has been registered.
steps.trigger: Add Zephyr's testimony and dream-related evidence to the notebook.

steps.objective: Interview Mariana Moon and locate the crew-position puzzle.
actions: Question Mariana about her historical research and the other passengers.
Search the sacks and collect the Silkwirm Map.
Inspect the large pinboard representing the submarine.
Leave the pinboard unfinished until every living crew member has been met.
steps.trigger: Return after interviewing Godrik Gripp and Madam Tadpole.

actions: Use the Glass Eyeball in the eye-shaped lock.
Push the inserted eye to release the internal key.
Inspect the broken handheld radio, table bell, ship in a bottle, and the painting titled Her.
Rotate every collected object and examine its hidden sides.
steps.trigger: Add Mortuga's private possessions to the evidence list.

steps.objective: Interview Godrik Gripp and repair the compass.
actions: Inspect three shop items to begin Gripp's full conversation.
Question him about the ship, Mortuga, his merchandise, and the crew.
Set the yellow compass to point left, the blue compass down, the pink compass toward roughly two o'clock, and the red compass toward the upper-left.
Collect the repaired compass.
steps.trigger: Present the repaired object and related evidence to the characters who recognize it.

actions: Ask Tadpole about her mermaid identity, Mortuga, and every suspect.
Record her account of a glowing-eyed figure on the Fore Deck.
Compare her description of its language with the shapes visible behind the cauldron.
Inspect the diving helmet and the other grotto equipment.
steps.trigger: All eight primary suspects have now been introduced.

actions: Place each living crew member at the room where they are currently stationed.
Place Mortuga's portrait outside the submarine diagram because he is dead.
Confirm the completed layout to establish the ship's current population and room structure.
steps.trigger: Additional testimony and personal-investigation topics become available.

actions: Show Mariana the pipe-shaped object and related mechanical evidence.
Establish that the pipe is a Dictaphone used to record crew conversations.
Listen to every available recording.
Present significant recorded statements to the people involved.
steps.trigger: New contradiction and motive topics appear in character interviews.

actions: Present each newly collected clue to all characters with an available reaction.
Revisit Sinthia and Symmetry after finding the photograph and Inn Deeds.
Revisit Dirk and Wirman after unlocking the phone and letter.
Revisit Zephyr, Mariana, Gripp, and Tadpole after solving their room puzzles.
Use the notebook to locate characters who still have unread evidence reactions.
steps.trigger: A character's personal investigation opens after the required testimony and evidence have been collected.

actions: Fill each conclusion with the statement supported by the collected testimony.
Attach the physical clue that directly proves the selected conclusion.
After an incorrect selection, use Grimoire and Sally's expanded comments to narrow the answer.
Repeat the interview loop whenever the game indicates that a relevant clue is still missing.
steps.trigger: Complete all required character investigations.

steps.location: Illusion Theatre and Final Case Sequence
actions: Finish all remaining room puzzles and evidence inspections before starting the confrontation.
Review the cauldron, stage, hidden mechanisms, crew movements, and Mortuga's possessions.
Build the timeline using only conclusions already proven in the personal investigations.
Present the matching physical evidence when each final deduction prompt appears.
steps.trigger: Confirm the completed murder reconstruction to enter the ending sequence.
steps.spoilerWarning: The culprit, method, and final revelations are intentionally omitted.

theMermaidMaskPuzzleSolutions.title: The Mermaid Mask Puzzle Solutions
theMermaidMaskPuzzleSolutions.subtitle: Find spoiler-controlled hints and direct answers for the submarine's object puzzles, locks, deduction devices, and investigation minigames.
theMermaidMaskPuzzleSolutions.intro: Most puzzles rely on rotating fully modeled objects, matching visual categories, or connecting evidence rather than entering traditional passwords. Open the hint first, then reveal the solution only when the mechanism remains unclear.

items.hint: Each shape represents its number of sides. Combine shapes when a display requires a larger total.
items.solution: Set 3 to one triangle, 9 to one square plus one pentagon, 7 to one triangle plus one square, and 4 to one square.
items.reward: Opens the cabinet and advances the route toward the crime scene.

items.hint: Patricia owns the medium-sized doll, while Jennifer and Margaret look toward her from opposite sides.
items.solution: Join the six cards into three complete girls. Put Patricia in the center and leave her doll unchanged. Use the magnifying and shrinking lenses on the two outer dolls, then place the fish-eye lenses over the faces of the left and right girls.

items.hint: Only three connections are needed, and each cable joins a socket on the upper row to one on the lower row.
items.solution: Connect the red cable from socket 1 to socket 9, the blue cable from socket 3 to socket 11, and the yellow cable from socket 6 to socket 12.

items.hint: Build continuous colored rings before worrying about the cords.
items.solution: Arrange all nine pieces into the hexagonal frame so the beveled edges form uninterrupted concentric bands. The magenta and red outer panels must surround a continuous yellow band, the purple inner wedges must face the center, and every cord must terminate at a matching peg or at the central joint.
items.reward: Completes the Control Room mechanism and registers its associated evidence.

items.hint: The numbers on connected branches are added to produce the fixed totals at their ends.
items.solution: Set the upper movable numbers to 3, 4, and 1 for a total of 8. Set the second row to 2, 3, and 1 for a total of 6. The three lower connected totals should read 5, 9, and 2.

items.hint: Every route has a symbol at both endpoints. The tiles between them must create one continuous matching channel.
items.solution: Solve the crossing routes first. Rotate and place each tile so channel A connects identical crossed symbols from left to right, while channels B, C, and D connect their matching top and bottom symbols without a broken line. Once all four routes are continuous, the phone unlocks.

items.hint: The five symbols are not separate pictures. Each is the previous pattern after a fixed rotation.
items.solution: Use the center preview to compare neighboring target symbols. Rotate the three inner dials and three outer rings so every adjacent pattern changes by 45 degrees while preserving the same component arrangement.
items.reward: Letter from Ibis to J.D. Wirman

items.hint: The prose clues describe visible or physical properties of the six bottles.
items.solution: Place the crescent cap on the bottle with the crimson-colored label, the spade cap on the effervescent bottle, the bird-skull cap on the bottle emitting vapor, the sun-shaped cap on the fast-dripping bottle, the hand cap on the viscous bottle, and the eye cap on the bottle with the conspicuous X.

items.hint: The column symbols describe head decorations. The row symbols describe facial features.
items.solution: Sort the skulls by ornament across the columns: red marking, side-marked skull, crescent marking, and horns. Sort them by face down the rows: narrow or closed eyes, prominent teeth or jaw, and large round eyes. Rotate each skull so the feature required by its row faces forward.
items.reward: Completes the skull display and reveals its associated clue.

items.hint: The suit determines the row, while the character's gaze, point, or orientation determines the column.
items.solution: Place every heart tile in the heart row, diamonds in the diamond row, clubs in the club row, and spades in the spade row. Within each row, order the tiles by the direction shown at the top of the column: upward, vertical, neutral, left, right, and downward.

items.hint: The board represents the submarine as it is now, not where people were during the murder.
items.solution: Place every living crew member in the room where they are currently stationed. Put Magnus Mortuga's portrait outside the submarine diagram because he is no longer aboard as a living occupant.
items.reward: Unlocks additional testimony and investigation topics.

items.hint: Rotate the light source and count how many mushrooms of each species glow under the required condition.
items.solution: Enter Dayglo 3, Evergleam 2, Illuminight 4, and Nevergleam 5.

items.hint: Each written clue describes how one compass should point in relation to the others.
items.solution: Set the yellow compass in the upper-left to point left, the blue compass in the lower-left to point down, the pink compass in the upper-right to point toward roughly two o'clock, and the red compass in the lower-right to point toward the upper-left.

items.hint: Treat every pipe as a route carrying one symbol between two dials.
items.solution: Begin with the central junction dials. Rotate each dial so the symbol leaving one pipe matches the symbol entering the next dial. Continue outward until every complete pipe route starts and ends with the same symbol.

items.hint: The small displays are target patterns. The large orbs must reproduce them when the colored layers overlap.
items.solution: Rotate the outer metal dials first to position the red groups, then rotate the inner glass orbs to align the white and pale spheres with the small reference displays. Confirm only when both large orbs reproduce their corresponding target arrangements.
items.reward: Opens the Bilge mechanism and registers its evidence.

items.hint: Match each pirate to the flag sharing its visual pattern, while lighthouse pieces occupy the remaining color-coded positions.
items.solution: Place the yellow lighthouse at the upper-left and the cyan lighthouse at the upper-right. Put the plain pale-yellow flag pirate between them on the left and the black-and-light-blue striped flag pirate between them on the right. Place the green-striped lighthouse at the lower-left and the red lighthouse at the lower-right. Match the remaining pirates to the red-and-white striped and black-white-green flags in the corresponding side and lower-center slots.

items.hint: The correct sentence must be supported by one specific clue, not merely by a suspicious statement.
items.solution: Complete the sentence using the testimony established during interviews, then attach the physical evidence that directly proves it. When an answer fails, read Grimoire and Sally's expanded response to identify whether the incorrect part is the statement or the supporting clue.
items.reward: Unlocks completed suspect conclusions and the final case sequence.

theMermaidMaskBeginnerGuide.title: The Mermaid Mask Beginner Guide
theMermaidMaskBeginnerGuide.subtitle: Learn how exploration, questioning, 3D evidence, deductions, and the built-in hint system work before beginning the Mortuga case.
theMermaidMaskBeginnerGuide.intro: The investigation advances by finding evidence and then showing it to the right people. There is no need to guess the culprit early: search each room carefully, inspect every side of each object, and revisit the crew whenever the notebook gains a meaningful clue.

items.title: Follow the Core Investigation Loop
items.summary: Every major section follows the same repeatable process.
tips: Explore every accessible room and inspect highlighted objects.
Question the people in that area about all available topics.
Present newly collected evidence to characters who may recognize it.
Return to earlier rooms when a clue reveals a hidden compartment or new interaction.
Use the resulting testimony and evidence in a personal investigation.

items.title: Use the Map Frequently
items.summary: The submarine expands gradually, and old rooms regularly receive new interactions.
tips: Check every unlocked floor after a major conversation.
Revisit the Parlor and crew quarters when an object appears to watch, blink, or react.
Do not assume a completed room is permanently finished.
Use room names in the notebook to track where unfinished evidence originated.

items.title: Exhaust Every Interview Topic
items.summary: A character's useful dialogue is divided between personal questions, suspect questions, and evidence reactions.
tips: Ask every introductory question when meeting someone for the first time.
Ask each person about every other crew member.
Present clues even when the connection is not immediately obvious.
Return after collecting photographs, letters, logs, recordings, or property documents.
New suspicion topics appear after enough supporting evidence has been seen.

items.title: Inspect 3D Evidence Completely
items.summary: Important clues are fully modeled objects that can hide information on their sides, back, base, or interior.
tips: Rotate each object through a full circle.
Zoom in on seams, labels, symbols, switches, and damaged areas.
Try opening lids, turning dials, moving parts, and reversing the object.
Continue until all inspection hotspots have been acknowledged.
Recheck an object when later testimony explains a symbol you previously ignored.

items.title: Read the Notebook Before Wandering
items.summary: The notebook records important evidence, testimony, rooms, and active lines of inquiry.
tips: Look for subjects that still lack a conclusion.
Compare a character's statement with the physical clues collected nearby.
Use unresolved entries to decide which suspect should be revisited.
Review the notebook after every multi-step puzzle or hidden-room discovery.

items.title: Build Deductions from Proof
items.summary: Personal investigations combine a completed statement with the clue that proves it.
tips: Choose the sentence that directly follows from established testimony.
Attach the most specific physical clue rather than a generally suspicious item.
Separate motive, opportunity, and method instead of treating them as one conclusion.
Read Grimoire and Sally's response after a failed attempt for an additional pointer.

items.title: Use Hints Without Penalty
items.summary: The optional hint system can guide the next action or explain a puzzle without affecting completion.
tips: Open the first hint when the objective is unclear.
Use deeper hints only after inspecting every part of the object.
Check whether the hint refers to a room, a person, or a deduction clue.
There is no score penalty for requesting help.

items.title: Recover from a Progression Block
items.summary: Most apparent dead ends are caused by one unread clue reaction or an incompletely inspected object.
tips: Rotate every recent 3D clue again and check for missed hotspots.
Present the newest evidence to all crew members with available dialogue.
Return to the room where that evidence was discovered.
Check the map for a newly opened floor or doorway.
Complete any unfinished personal investigation shown in the notebook.

items.title: Avoid Accidental Story Spoilers
items.summary: The case is designed so players can investigate freely without knowing the final solution.
tips: Use chapter- and room-specific hints instead of reading the ending route.
Finish each suspect investigation before opening final-case material.
Keep the final confrontation section collapsed until all normal dialogue is exhausted.
Treat suspicious statements as leads rather than proof of guilt.

theMermaidMaskCharactersAndSuspects.title: The Mermaid Mask Characters and Suspects
theMermaidMaskCharactersAndSuspects.subtitle: Meet Detective Grimoire, Sally, the murdered captain, and the eight eccentric people investigated aboard the Mortuga submarine.
theMermaidMaskCharactersAndSuspects.intro: Every crew member reacts to evidence and to the other suspects, making repeat interviews essential. These dossiers cover their public roles, relationships, testimony, investigative angles, and early evidence without naming the culprit or revealing the final solution.

items.name: Detective Grimoire
relationships: Longtime investigative partner of Sally Spears
Hired to investigate the death of Magnus Mortuga
items.testimony: Grimoire builds the case by inspecting physical evidence, questioning the crew, and completing evidence-supported deductions.
evidence: All collected testimony and physical clues

items.name: Sally Spears
items.role: Grimoire's sharp-tongued assistant and investigative partner
relationships: Closest ally of Detective Grimoire
Questions and comments on the crew's behavior throughout the case
items.testimony: Sally provides observations, challenges weak deductions, and gives increasingly specific feedback after unsuccessful puzzle or conclusion attempts.

items.name: Magnus Mortuga
items.role: Murdered captain and owner of the Mortuga submarine
items.location: Found dead in the Illusion Theatre
relationships: Brought the unusual crew together aboard his submarine
Connected to Sinthia Seafoam through the ship and its command
Previously associated with the Stonetop Inn property
items.testimony: Mortuga cannot testify, so his history must be reconstructed through his log, possessions, property records, and the statements of his crew.
items.motive: The victim's private dealings, ownership history, and relationships provide possible motives for several suspects.
items.spoiler: Contains early case evidence but no ending revelations.

items.name: Sinthia Seafoam
items.role: Acting captain and experienced nautical engineer
relationships: Friend and bunkmate of Symmetry Silkmoth
Succeeded Mortuga as the submarine's acting captain
Worked around ships and nautical machinery for many years
items.testimony: Sinthia discusses the ship, Mortuga's command, her engineering background, the crew, and the responsibilities she inherited after his death.
items.motive: Her succession to command, concealed photograph, and connection to Mortuga's property history make her secrecy an important investigative angle.
items.spoiler: Minor investigation spoilers; final conclusions are omitted.

items.name: Symmetry Silkmoth
relationships: Friend and bunkmate of Sinthia Seafoam
Connected to the submarine's theatrical spaces and illusion imagery
items.testimony: Symmetry answers questions about stagecraft, the crew, Mortuga, the strange newspaper story, the sail cloth, the illusion poster, and the Glass Eyeball.
items.motive: Her knowledge of illusions, concealed spaces, and visual deception makes her relevant to an apparently impossible locked-room murder.
items.spoiler: Minor room and clue spoilers; final conclusions are omitted.

items.name: Madam Tadpole
items.role: Self-described half-mermaid and resident of the Airlock Grotto
relationships: Lives apart from much of the crew in the submarine's aquatic area
Provides testimony that places an unidentified figure on the Fore Deck
items.testimony: Tadpole says she saw a glowing-eyed demon on the Fore Deck around the time of the murder and heard it speak what she describes as an alien language.
items.motive: Her access to the airlock, unusual account, and knowledge of activity outside the main rooms make her movements important to the timeline.
items.spoiler: Contains testimony revealed during the normal first interview.

items.name: Dr. Zacharias Zephyr
relationships: Offers dream-related services to people aboard the submarine
His appearance and equipment become relevant to Tadpole's testimony
items.testimony: Zephyr explains his dream practice, discusses Mortuga and the crew, and comments on the unusual devices kept in his chamber.
items.motive: His mysterious equipment, chemical bottles, and possible connection to unusual perceptions place his methods under scrutiny.
items.spoiler: Minor evidence spoilers; final conclusions are omitted.

items.name: J.D. Wirman
items.role: Successful writer and author connected to The Hex of the Helmsmen
relationships: Shares the men's quarters with Dirk Dansom
Receives concealed correspondence from a person signing as Ibis
items.testimony: Wirman discusses his writing, his status aboard the ship, Mortuga, Dirk, and the other members of the crew.
items.motive: His secret correspondence and literary connection to the case's supernatural imagery raise questions about what he knew before the murder.
items.spoiler: Minor correspondence spoilers; the letter's final significance is omitted.

items.name: Dirk Dansom
relationships: Shares the men's quarters with J.D. Wirman
Appears in entertainment material found aboard the submarine
items.testimony: Dirk discusses his career, Mortuga, Wirman, and the rest of the crew. His locked phone contains private calls, messages, and emails.
items.motive: Private communications and a secretly recorded statement about killing someone to become free suggest that he was under personal pressure.
items.spoiler: Includes a minor recorded-dialogue spoiler but not its final interpretation.

items.name: Mariana Moon
items.role: Historian and meticulous collector of information
relationships: Studies the submarine, its occupants, and their history
Secretly records conversations among the crew
items.testimony: Mariana discusses Mortuga's history, the crew, her research, the Silkwirm material, and the objects stored in the Cargo Hold.
items.motive: Her hidden recordings show that she possessed information other suspects did not know she had, making her knowledge and timeline important.
items.spoiler: Minor recording spoilers; final conclusions are omitted.

items.name: Godrik "Mr" Gripp
relationships: Trades and repairs unusual objects for the submarine's occupants
Has direct access to machinery and navigational equipment
items.testimony: Gripp discusses his merchandise, mechanical work, Mortuga, and the other crew members after Grimoire examines the items in his shop.
items.motive: His access to the submarine's mechanical systems and his work with damaged or altered objects create questions about means and opportunity.
items.spoiler: Minor mechanical-clue spoilers; final conclusions are omitted.

theMermaidMaskCluesAndEvidence.title: The Mermaid Mask Clues and Evidence
theMermaidMaskCluesAndEvidence.subtitle: Track the physical objects, documents, testimony, recordings, and environmental details needed to solve Captain Mortuga's locked-room murder.
theMermaidMaskCluesAndEvidence.intro: Evidence aboard the Mortuga submarine often becomes useful only after it is fully examined and presented to the correct crew member. Use this table to find each clue, identify the suspects connected to it, and see whether it is required for progression or an optional investigation detail.

items.name: Case File
items.foundBy: Open the red case folder before leaving the inn.
items.examination: Read the locked-room murder summary and review the known facts about Magnus Mortuga's death.
items.unlocks: Introduces the murder case and the initial investigation objective.

items.name: The Hex of the Helmsmen
items.foundBy: Inspect the book lying near Sally.
items.examination: Read its supernatural maritime story and compare its imagery with later references to curses, masks, and the cauldron.
items.unlocks: Provides thematic context for the apparent curse surrounding the murder.

items.name: Unusual Green Light
items.foundBy: Inspect the glowing area before the submarine surfaces.
items.examination: Observe the color, location, and Sally's reaction to the light.
items.unlocks: Advances the arrival sequence and introduces the submarine's unusual visual technology.

items.name: Glowing Carved Buoy
items.foundBy: Inspect the carved buoy immediately after boarding.
items.examination: Rotate the view and compare its glowing details with later descriptions of lights seen outside the submarine.
items.unlocks: Adds context to later Fore Deck testimony.

items.name: Ragged Sail Cloth
items.foundBy: Inspect the torn fabric near the stage and crime scene.
items.examination: Check the damaged edges, material, and its connection to The Stargazing Girl display.
items.unlocks: Supports questions about stage equipment, concealment, and movement inside the theatre.

items.name: Upside-Down Illusion Poster
items.foundBy: Inspect the theatre poster from both orientations.
items.examination: Turn the image upside down and identify how its meaning changes when viewed differently.
items.unlocks: Establishes visual deception as an important part of the case.

items.name: Chalk Outline
items.foundBy: Inspect every highlighted point around Mortuga's body position.
items.examination: Compare the body's position with the cauldron, stage entrances, and nearby mechanisms.
items.unlocks: Supports the first deduction about how the victim died.

items.name: Stone Cauldron
items.foundBy: Examine the ancient cauldron beside the chalk outline.
items.examination: Inspect the lock, rim, interior, exterior symbols, base, and surrounding stage connections.
items.unlocks: Allows Grimoire to conclude that Mortuga opened the cauldron and was killed by what emerged from or was concealed inside it.

items.name: Lady of the Looking Glass Newspaper
items.foundBy: Read both sides of the newspaper page.
items.examination: Review the illusion story on the front and the sea-serpent report printed on the reverse.
items.unlocks: Creates new interview topics concerning illusions and supposed sea creatures.

items.name: Glass Eyeball
items.foundBy: Complete the portrait-card and lens mechanism.
items.examination: Rotate the glass eye and inspect its shape, iris, rear fitting, and mechanical edges.
items.unlocks: Opens the eye-shaped lock on the Captain's Cabin.

items.name: Submarine Blueprint
items.foundBy: Inspect the plans displayed in the Control Room.
items.examination: Review the submarine's floors, connected rooms, restricted sections, and possible movement routes.
items.unlocks: Provides context for room access, machinery, and the crew-location investigation.

items.name: Captain's Log
items.foundBy: Solve the switchboard by connecting red 1 to 9, blue 3 to 11, and yellow 6 to 12.
items.examination: Read Mortuga's entries and compare them with the crew's accounts of his recent actions.
items.unlocks: Adds new questions about Mortuga's plans, command decisions, and private concerns.

items.name: Gloomy Underwater Photograph
items.foundBy: Open the pink pig safe and collect the concealed photograph.
items.examination: Inspect the figures, setting, background, lighting, and any identifying features.
items.unlocks: Creates new reactions from Sinthia and Symmetry and points toward another hidden item.

items.name: Inn Deeds
items.foundBy: Search beneath Sinthia's mattress after discussing the photograph with Symmetry.
items.examination: Read the ownership names, property details, dates, and connections to Stonetop Inn.
items.unlocks: Reveals a concealed financial and personal connection involving Mortuga.

items.name: Dirk's Phone
items.foundBy: Complete the matching-symbol channel puzzle.
items.examination: Read every available call record, message, and email after unlocking the device.
items.unlocks: Adds private statements and relationship details to Dirk's investigation.

items.name: Letter from Ibis
items.foundBy: Solve the rotating typewriter-ring mechanism.
items.examination: Read the full handwritten message and inspect the signature, wording, and intended recipient.
items.unlocks: Introduces a secret relationship and new questions about Wirman's knowledge before the murder.

items.name: Hex Bottle
items.foundBy: Inspect Zephyr's bottle collection and complete the cap-matching puzzle.
items.examination: Rotate each bottle and compare its liquid, label, vapor, viscosity, and markings with the available caps.
items.unlocks: Supports questions about altered perception, chemicals, dreams, and supernatural claims.

items.name: Silkwirm Map
items.foundBy: Search the stored sacks and materials in the Cargo Hold.
items.examination: Inspect its routes, labels, symbols, and any locations connected to Mortuga's history.
items.unlocks: Expands Mariana's historical testimony and the background of the submarine's voyage.

items.name: Pipe-Shaped Dictaphone
items.foundBy: Present the suspicious pipe-shaped object to Mariana after collecting the relevant mechanical evidence.
items.examination: Identify its concealed recording function and play every stored conversation.
items.unlocks: Reveals statements the speakers did not know had been recorded and creates new contradiction topics.

items.name: Broken Handheld Radio
items.foundBy: Open the Captain's Cabin with the Glass Eyeball and inspect Mortuga's table.
items.examination: Rotate the radio, inspect its damaged casing, controls, antenna, and internal components.
items.unlocks: Supports questions about communication, equipment damage, and Mortuga's final activities.

items.name: Painting Titled Her
items.foundBy: Inspect the framed painting in Mortuga's private room.
items.examination: Study the depicted figure, title, frame, background, and Mortuga's reason for keeping it.
items.unlocks: Adds personal context to Mortuga's past and the emotional meaning of the case.

items.name: Repaired Compass
items.foundBy: Solve the four-compass direction puzzle in Gripp's shop.
items.examination: Inspect its casing, needle, repaired sections, and signs of previous damage or modification.
items.unlocks: Creates additional reactions from characters who recognize the object or its origin.

items.name: Diving Helmet
items.foundBy: Inspect the helmet and complete its connected pipe-dial mechanism.
items.examination: Rotate the helmet, trace each pipe route, and inspect the eye pieces, seals, and breathing components.
items.unlocks: Provides context for the glowing-eyed figure reportedly seen on the Fore Deck.

items.name: Crew Location Pinboard
items.foundBy: Return after meeting every living crew member.
items.examination: Place each person in their current room and position Mortuga outside the living-occupant layout.
items.unlocks: Establishes the submarine's current population and opens additional personal investigations.

theMermaidMaskAchievementsGuide.title: The Mermaid Mask Achievements Guide
theMermaidMaskAchievementsGuide.subtitle: Plan a complete investigation, finish every important puzzle, inspect optional evidence, and earn all 21 Steam achievements with minimal cleanup.
theMermaidMaskAchievementsGuide.intro: The Steam release contains 21 achievements covering story progress, room puzzles, evidence inspection, interviews, and full-case completion. The descriptive achievement labels below identify each unlock route without exposing late-game revelations; hidden story achievements unlock naturally when the required investigation sequence is completed.

items.name: The Beginning
items.description: Complete the opening sequence at Stonetop Inn and begin the journey to the Mortuga submarine.
items.howToUnlock: Read the Case File, inspect The Hex of the Helmsmen, wake Sally, and leave the inn.
items.cleanup: Unlocks automatically during normal story progression.

items.name: All Aboard
items.description: Board the Mortuga submarine after investigating the unusual light in Town Square.
items.howToUnlock: Inspect the square, speak with Sally, wait for the submarine to surface, and enter from the Fore Deck.
items.cleanup: Unlocks automatically before the first interior investigation.

items.name: Cabinet Cracker
items.description: Open the snake cabinet in the Mess Hall.
items.howToUnlock: Treat each shape as its number of sides and enter the values 3, 9, 7, and 4 using the available shape combinations.
items.cleanup: Required for story progression.

items.name: The Curse in the Cauldron
items.description: Complete the opening crime-scene deduction in the Illusion Theatre.
items.howToUnlock: Inspect the chalk outline, ragged cloth, illusion poster, stage, and cauldron before confirming the first conclusion.
items.cleanup: Unlocks automatically when Chapter One begins.

items.name: Eye for Detail
items.description: Complete the portrait-card and lens mechanism in the Child's Room.
items.howToUnlock: Assemble the three girls, place Patricia in the center, resize the outer dolls, and use the fish-eye lenses on the outer faces.
items.cleanup: Required to obtain the Glass Eyeball.

items.name: Switchboard Specialist
items.description: Restore the Control Room switchboard and recover the Captain's Log.
items.howToUnlock: Connect the red cable from 1 to 9, blue from 3 to 11, and yellow from 6 to 12.
items.cleanup: Required for the main investigation.

items.name: Safe and Sound
items.description: Open the pink pig safe in the Women's Bunk Room.
items.howToUnlock: Arrange the branch values so the upper totals equal 8 and 6 and the lower connected totals equal 5, 9, and 2.
items.cleanup: Complete it before finishing Sinthia's personal investigation.

items.name: Private Caller
items.description: Unlock Dirk Dansom's phone and read its stored communications.
items.howToUnlock: Rotate the channel tiles until every route connects matching endpoint symbols, then open all calls, messages, and emails.
items.cleanup: Read every phone section before leaving the device interface.

items.name: Written in Code
items.description: Solve the typewriter-ring mechanism in the Men's Bunk Room.
items.howToUnlock: Rotate the inner and outer rings until the five patterns follow the required 45-degree transformations.
items.cleanup: Required to recover the letter addressed to Wirman.

items.name: Sweet Dreams
items.description: Complete the bottle-cap puzzle in Dr. Zephyr's Dream Chamber.
items.howToUnlock: Match the crescent, spade, bird-skull, sun, hand, and eye caps to the bottles described by their visible and physical properties.
items.cleanup: Finish the mechanism while reviewing all Dream Chamber evidence.

items.name: Curiosity Satisfied
items.description: Complete the skull classification display in the Cabinet of Curiosity.
items.howToUnlock: Sort each skull by its ornament and facial feature, then rotate it so the required identifying feature faces forward.
items.cleanup: Complete optional room mechanisms before entering the final confrontation.

items.name: Know Your Crew
items.description: Meet and question every primary suspect aboard the submarine.
items.howToUnlock: Complete the initial interviews with Sinthia, Symmetry, Dirk, Wirman, Zephyr, Mariana, Gripp, and Madam Tadpole.
items.cleanup: Ask every introductory topic when first meeting each character.

items.name: A Place for Everyone
items.description: Complete the crew-location pinboard in the Cargo Hold.
items.howToUnlock: Place every living crew member in their current room and position Mortuga outside the submarine's living-occupant diagram.
items.cleanup: Return to the board after all eight suspects have been introduced.

items.name: Recorded for Posterity
items.description: Identify Mariana Moon's disguised Dictaphone and listen to its recordings.
items.howToUnlock: Present the pipe-shaped object with the relevant mechanical evidence, then play every available recorded conversation.
items.cleanup: Listen to every recording before completing the related personal investigations.

items.name: True North
items.description: Repair the compass mechanism in the Engine Room.
items.howToUnlock: Point yellow left, blue down, pink toward roughly two o'clock, and red toward the upper-left.
items.cleanup: Collect the repaired compass and present it to characters with new reactions.

items.name: Deep-Sea Diver
items.description: Complete the diving-helmet mechanism in the Airlock Grotto.
items.howToUnlock: Rotate every connected dial so each pipe route begins and ends with the same symbol.
items.cleanup: Finish it during Madam Tadpole's investigation rather than postponing it.

items.name: Evidence Collector
items.description: Register every required physical clue used by the main investigation.
items.howToUnlock: Collect documents, photographs, recordings, mechanical objects, and crime-scene evidence from every accessible room.
items.cleanup: Inspect each room again before the final sequence and verify that no required evidence entry remains incomplete.

items.name: Master Interrogator
items.description: Exhaust the important interview and evidence-reaction topics for the entire crew.
items.howToUnlock: Ask each suspect about every crew member and present all major documents, photographs, recordings, and mechanical clues.
items.cleanup: After every major discovery, revisit all characters showing unread dialogue indicators.

items.name: No Stone Unturned
items.description: Fully inspect the submarine's interactive rooms and three-dimensional evidence objects.
items.howToUnlock: Rotate each object through every angle, activate all hotspots, and complete the optional mechanisms in rooms such as the Bilge, Dome Tank, and Cabinet of Curiosity.
items.cleanup: Perform a complete room sweep before selecting the final-case confrontation.

items.name: Case Closed
items.description: Complete the final reconstruction of Captain Magnus Mortuga's murder.
items.howToUnlock: Finish the required personal investigations, build the final timeline, and present the matching physical evidence during the confrontation.
items.cleanup: Unlocks automatically when the main story is completed.

items.name: Perfect Investigation
items.description: Complete the story while resolving the optional investigation content needed for full achievement completion.
items.howToUnlock: Finish every required puzzle, complete optional room mechanisms, inspect all important evidence, and exhaust the significant crew reactions before closing the case.
items.cleanup: Use a save made before the final confrontation to finish missed inspections or optional puzzles without replaying the full game.

theMermaidMaskEndingExplained.title: The Mermaid Mask Ending Explained
theMermaidMaskEndingExplained.subtitle: Understand how the Illusion Theatre murder, the cauldron, the submarine's hidden mechanisms, and the crew's overlapping secrets fit into the final reconstruction.
theMermaidMaskEndingExplained.intro: The ending resolves the apparent supernatural curse by combining the evidence collected across the Mortuga submarine with the crew's hidden relationships and contradictory testimony. The sections below explain the logic of the final case while separating the murder method from the unrelated secrets that made several suspects appear guilty.

items.question: Why does Mortuga's death initially appear supernatural?
items.answer: Mortuga is found beside an ancient cauldron inside the Illusion Theatre after apparently unlocking it himself. The curse imagery from The Hex of the Helmsmen, the strange symbols, Tadpole's account of a glowing-eyed figure, and the theatrical setting encourage the crew to believe that something inhuman emerged from the cauldron.

items.question: What does the opening deduction establish?
items.answer: Grimoire establishes that Magnus Mortuga personally unlocked the cauldron and that the immediate cause of his death came from whatever was concealed, released, or presented as emerging from inside it. This becomes the foundation of the final reconstruction rather than proof that the curse was real.

items.question: How does the Illusion Theatre create a locked-room mystery?
items.answer: The theatre is designed around misdirection. The upside-down poster, ragged stage cloth, hidden mechanisms, lighting, movable displays, and the cauldron's construction demonstrate that an observer can be shown one version of an event while the real movement or preparation occurs out of sight.

items.question: Why are the cauldron symbols and glowing-eye testimony important?
items.answer: The symbols behind the cauldron and Madam Tadpole's description of an alien language connect two events that initially seem supernatural. When compared with equipment such as the diving helmet, Dream Chamber devices, submarine lighting, and other mechanical clues, the testimony becomes evidence of disguise, distorted perception, or deliberate staging rather than a literal demon.

items.question: What is the importance of Mortuga's private possessions?
items.answer: The Captain's Log, Inn Deeds, broken radio, captain's jacket, ship model, table bell, and painting titled Her reveal that Mortuga's public role as an eccentric captain concealed personal commitments, ownership disputes, private communications, and emotional ties. These details explain why multiple people had reasons to confront him even though only one final reconstruction explains the murder.

items.question: Why do so many crew members look guilty?
items.answer: Nearly every suspect hides something unrelated to the killing. Sinthia conceals property information, Symmetry understands stage deception, Dirk has troubling private communications, Wirman receives secret correspondence, Mariana records the crew, Zephyr owns perception-altering equipment, Gripp handles machinery, and Tadpole withholds an unusual sighting. The case works by separating secrecy from murder evidence.

items.question: What do Mariana's recordings change?
items.answer: The Dictaphone preserves conversations that the speakers believed were private. These recordings expose contradictions, pressure, resentments, and statements that sound incriminating when isolated. Grimoire must compare each recording with physical evidence and the established timeline instead of treating dramatic language as a confession.

items.question: How do the personal investigations lead to the final answer?
items.answer: Each personal investigation proves one limited conclusion about a suspect's actions, secrets, opportunity, or relationship with Mortuga. Once these smaller conclusions are supported by specific evidence, Grimoire can eliminate explanations that depend only on suspicion and combine the remaining facts into a single murder timeline.

items.question: What role does Sally play in the resolution?
items.answer: Sally repeatedly challenges conclusions that are emotionally convincing but unsupported. During the final sequence, her observations help distinguish what the crew assumed they saw from what the room's evidence proves. Her feedback also prevents Grimoire from confusing a hidden motive with the physical method used in the theatre.

items.question: What does the Mermaid Mask represent?
items.answer: The mermaid imagery represents the boundary between an identity someone performs and the truth hidden underneath it. Masks, costumes, stage illusions, dream equipment, theatrical stories, and Mortuga's legends all allow characters to control how others see them. The final case removes those constructed identities one piece at a time.

items.question: Is the curse real?
items.answer: The investigation treats the curse as part of the murderer's presentation rather than a replacement for evidence. Every decisive conclusion depends on physical objects, room access, recorded testimony, mechanical systems, and proven character movements. The supernatural atmosphere remains important to the story, but the murder is solved through observable facts.

items.question: What is the ending's main message?
items.answer: The ending argues that suspicious behavior is not the same as guilt and that dramatic stories can hide ordinary human motives. Grimoire and Sally solve the case by examining every object from more than one angle, which mirrors the game's recurring visual puzzles and its broader theme of looking beyond the first interpretation.

faq.title: The Mermaid Mask
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know before investigating The Mermaid Mask

questions.question: What kind of game is The Mermaid Mask?
questions.answer: The Mermaid Mask is a single-player, hand-painted point-and-click murder mystery. You play Detective Grimoire and solve Captain Mortuga's locked-room murder aboard the Mortuga submarine.

questions.question: Is The Mermaid Mask singleplayer only?
questions.answer: Yes. The Mermaid Mask is a single-player story adventure. Sally accompanies Grimoire as a story character, but there is no multiplayer or co-op mode.

questions.question: Where can I play The Mermaid Mask?
questions.answer: The Mermaid Mask launched on July 16, 2026 on PC via Steam, Nintendo Switch, Nintendo Switch 2, and PlayStation 5.

questions.question: How long is The Mermaid Mask?
questions.answer: The Mermaid Mask is a focused detective case. Most players complete the main investigation in a single sitting, with extra time needed to inspect optional evidence and earn all 21 Steam achievements.

questions.question: Does The Mermaid Mask have achievements?
questions.answer: Yes. The Steam release of The Mermaid Mask includes 21 achievements covering story progress, room puzzles, evidence inspection, interviews, and full-case completion.

questions.question: How do puzzles work in The Mermaid Mask?
questions.answer: Most puzzles in The Mermaid Mask rely on rotating fully modeled 3D objects, matching visual categories, or connecting evidence rather than entering traditional passwords. An optional hint system explains the next step without penalty.

questions.question: Which languages does The Mermaid Mask support?
questions.answer: The Mermaid Mask supports 12 languages. Check the selected storefront for the current localized dialogue and investigation text.

questions.question: Is The Mermaid Mask part of a series?
questions.answer: Yes. The Mermaid Mask is a Detective Grimoire game from SFB Games, continuing the detective adventure series that includes Tangle Tower.
